title: Using volume mount subpath with SELinux labelling
body:
### Feature request description

I would like to mount a volume into a container. I need both the subpath feature and the automatic SELinux labelling feature. But there doesn't seem to be a way to do both, seemingly due to a limitation in CLI options.

To use subpath, I need to use the `--mount` flag with the `subpath` option; to use automatic SELinux relabelling, I need to use the `--volume` flag with the `z` or `Z` option. I want both.

### Suggest potential solution

Either:

- add a `subpath` option to the `--volume` flag
- or add `z` and `Z` options to the `--mount` flag

Or ideally both.

### Have you considered any alternatives?

Treat it as if those two features are completely incompatible and only use one of them at a time. Not ideal.

### Additional context

N/A

comments.body:
That is already supported.
--mount uses `relabel=shared` or `relabel=private` see the examples but it can also use `z/Z` 
https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/markdown/podman-create.1.html#mount-type-type-type-specific-option

`--mount type=volume,src=test,dst=/test,subpath=dir,relabel=private` or `--mount type=volume,src=test,dst=/test,subpath=dir,Z`  both seem to work just fine

Ah right sorry I missed it. I must have grepped for `selinux|z` and didn't find anything.

~~Would you like me to submit a PR that slightly elaborates on the documentation for the `--mount` option?~~ The goal is to make it more discoverable.

I realised I just ran afoul of [don't ask to ask](https://dontasktoask.com/) (not to mention it made me sound like an LLM). I'll just submit the PR.
